admissions.role: admission warrant with eight binding conditions

commits.role: Slice A implementation: record module, capture module, wiring, settings rider, tests, registry and documentation
First real capture: COMP-0035 and two InvocationRecords
Capture fidelity fixes from first-run findings

files.role: capture surface for the admitted path
schema, rejection class, condition, and integration tests
predicate registry for the seven Slice A predicates
record family documentation and stated limitations

interfaces: ai_lab.providers.invocation_record.build_invocation_record (assemble and validate a Slice A record)
ai_lab.providers.invocation_capture.capture_provider_invocation (capture on the admitted path, returns record and error)
limits: Capture is restricted to scripts/compare_providers.py. No other path emits records, and the validator rejects records claiming any other capture_path.

completeness_attestation is partial_declared_channels_only on this path: the manifest is not declared exhaustive for all effective-input channels, so no captured record qualifies as blind-witness evidence.
identity_verification_status is unresolved for every model executor; no resolution mechanism exists until Slice B.
governance_marker defaults to experimental and stays there until an admission consumer exists.
Relations are emitted with authoritative false and relation_source future_edge_seed; they are diagnostic seeds, not authoritative graph edges.
Nothing reads the records. C1 provenance_v1 activation, catalog resolution, roles and authorization, and lineage and admission remain unimplemented.
Records are prospective only; invocations before this capability fall under the ABS-0004 Section 7 pre-provenance boundary.
name: Invocation provenance capture (ABS-0004 Slice A)
recommended_next_actions: Propose Slice B (catalog identity resolution) against GAP-0005, incorporating the COMP-0035 finding that naming a provider self-report CatalogVerification is circular self-attestation.
Decide whether ABS-0004 requires a v5 amendment renaming CatalogVerification or scoping the term to what endpoint authentication actually proves.
Consider a check that execution-profile fidelity has not silently degraded after provider refactors.
risks: Schema v1 field choices are load-bearing for Slices B to D; a field misdesign propagates, mitigated by copying ABS-0004 v4 names verbatim and by the enumerated identity subset that isolates later additions.
Capture depends on reading private provider attributes for execution configuration; a provider refactor could silently reduce profile fidelity, which no current check would catch.
The governed rider raised the Claude output-token default to 16000, increasing the cost ceiling per call.
summary: Provider calls on the scripts/compare_providers.py path emit atomic InvocationRecords in schema v1: executor reference with identity verification status, requested API model name, service endpoint, session identity, occurred_at, status, governance marker, spawned edges, a minimal EffectiveInputManifest (rendered prompt digest, instruction and tool-schema digests, ContextManifest reference, prior tool-result references, session-state mode, completeness attestation), and an ExecutionProfile (endpoint, requested model name, output-token limit and its source, sampling and reasoning parameters, request flags, SDK runtime version). Records are validated by validate_invocation_record, serialized as byte-stable canonical JSON at deterministic paths under docs/invocations/, and identified by a digest over the enumerated version-scoped subset IDENTITY_FIELDS_V1 so that later slices cannot alter existing identities. Relations are emitted only in GraphRelation shape and only for the seven ABS-0004 Section 5 Slice A predicates. Capture is additive: failures are reported and never abort a provider call, and no consumer reads the records.
